# vercel_blob_project_connection (Resource)

Provides a project connection for a Vercel Blob store.

This resource links an existing Blob store to a project and manages the generated `BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` environment variable prefix and target environments.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "vercel_blob_store" "example" {
  name = "example-blob-store"
}

resource "vercel_project" "example" {
  name = "example-blob-project"
}

resource "vercel_blob_project_connection" "example" {
  blob_store_id = vercel_blob_store.example.id
  project_id    = vercel_project.example.id
  environments  = ["preview", "production"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `blob_store_id` (String) The ID of the Blob store to connect.
- `project_id` (String) The ID of the Vercel project to connect to the Blob store.

### Optional

- `env_var_prefix` (String) The prefix used for the generated Blob environment variable names.
- `environments` (Set of String) The environments in which the generated Blob environment variables should be created.
- `team_id` (String) The ID of the team that owns the Blob store and project. Required when configuring a team resource if a default team has not been set in the provider.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of the Blob store project connection.
- `read_write_token_env_var_name` (String) The generated environment variable name that contains the Blob read/write token.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# If importing into a personal account, or with a team configured on
# the provider, use the Blob store id and connection id.
terraform import vercel_blob_project_connection.example store_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx/spc_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# Alternatively, you can import via the team_id, Blob store id, and connection id.
terraform import vercel_blob_project_connection.example team_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx/store_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx/spc_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
```
